global_constraints.description: No secrets or credentials in the produced diff
global_constraints.expression: git diff has no credential patterns

global_constraints.description: All modified files stay within the repository (no escape)

criteria: Treat the input as INTENT. A good spec, authored from it, restates the desired outcome in one or two sentences — not a copy of the raw issue.
WARN if no explicit acceptance criterion is present (how we'll know it's resolved).
WARN if scope is unstated (which area/behavior the change should touch).
WARN if the spec is code-prescriptive (transcribed implementation) rather than intent + constraints.
Do not block: these are good-manners nudges meant to improve the spec on the next iteration, not stop the work.
You are reviewing a CODE DIFF that attempts to resolve the issue described in the task. Judge the diff, not the wording of the task.
BLOCK if the diff is empty, unrelated to the issue, or clearly fails to address the described problem.
BLOCK if the change introduces obvious breakage: syntax errors, undefined names, or removal of unrelated functionality.
The fix should be minimal and focused on the issue; flag large unrelated rewrites as a concern.
Do NOT require the author to add or modify tests — correctness is verified separately. Judge only whether the change plausibly and cleanly resolves the issue.
